pi_items.source: setup_task.sh lines 36, 70 create order with uid=2; verifier.py line 28 checks for expected_uid=2
task.json description, setup_task.sh line 3 comment 'Creates a placed order for johndoe', export_result.sh line 82 searches for 'REFUND-JOHNDOE', verifier.py line 63 references johndoe

Calculated from seed_products.php: Apple MacBook Air M2 13-inch (line 46, $1099.00) + Keychron Q1 Pro Mechanical Keyboard (line 52, $199.00) = $1298.00. Confirmed in task description and setup_task.sh line 118.
Task description states 'the order total', which is $1,298.00. Confirmed in setup_task.sh line 118: 'Order #$ORDER_ID created for johndoe (completed state, $1,298.00 total)'
task.json description line 5: 'create a coupon with code 'REFUND-JOHNDOE''. export_result.sh line 82 queries for code='REFUND-JOHNDOE'

seed_products.php: Line 46 defines 'Apple MacBook Air M2 13-inch' with sku='APPLE-MBA-M2-13' ($1099.00). Line 52 defines 'Keychron Q1 Pro Mechanical Keyboard' with sku='KEYCHRON-Q1PRO' ($199.00). setup_task.sh uses variation_id 2 (MacBook) and 8 (Keychron).

privileged_info_summary: Task process_customer_refund involves a Drupal Commerce environment managing an order for customer johndoe (uid=2). The order contains two products: Apple MacBook Air M2 13-inch (APPLE-MBA-M2-13, $1099.00) and Keychron Q1 Pro Mechanical Keyboard (KEYCHRON-Q1PRO, $199.00), totaling $1298.00. The customer requires store credit of $1298.00, issued via promotion code REFUND-JOHNDOE with usage limit 1. All data is synthetic, created by task setup scripts in a test Drupal Commerce environment.
